sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Establishing a SAFE STREETS Citizen Oversight Committee (SSCOC) to review
expenditures and progress reports from the City Manager and City Auditor to
ensure compliance with the intent of Measure FF, passed by voters on
November 5, 2024, evaluating the impacts and outcomes of tax expenditures on
stated goals and prepare public reports on the Committee’s findings; and
2. Authorizing the City Manager to further implement Section 7.11.040 of City
Ordinance 7,952-N.S, to ensure compliance with all requirements.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The SSCOC will require Public Works staff time to manage the scheduling, public
facility usage, communications, agendas, progress reports and Council reporting
processes. An existing fulltime Public Works staff member will be appointed by the
Director to serve as Committee Secretary, managing the SSCOC’s operational and
communication needs.
sections.current_situation:
Requirements for establishing the SSCOC through Council Resolution are explicitly laid
out in Section 7.11.040 of City Ordinance 7,952-N.S, with a specific membership
composition detailed in Section 7.11.040 (B):
B. Composition:
1. The SSCOC shall consist of seven members, as follows:
a. Two members of the Transportation and Infrastructure
Commission
b. One member of the Environment and Climate Commission
c. Four members of the public who are Berkeley residents
2. SSCOC members from commissions shall be appointed by their
respective bodies, and members of the public shall be appointed by

sections.background:
Measure FF, also known as the known as the Sustainable and Accountable Funding for
Equitable Street Transformation, Revitalization, Enhancements, and Essential Traffic
Safety (SAFE STREETS) Initiative, was passed by voters on November 5, 2025, and
adopted as Ordinance 7,952-N.S. on December 10, 2025, requires the establishment of
the SSCOC in Section 7.11.040.
The SAFE STREETS Initiative authorizes an annual parcel tax of $0.17 per square foot
for residential properties and $0.25 per square foot for other properties over a 14-year
period. This measure is projected to generate approximately $15 million annually,
dedicated to funding street and sidewalk repairs, safety improvements, and
environmental infrastructure projects.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
All City departments strive to reduce paper usage. The SSCOC will strive to operate
with a digital document retention practice, rather than traditional paper records, unless
otherwise required for accessibility purposes.
sections.rationale:
The City Manager and Council are required by Ordinance 7,952-N.S. to establish the
SSCOC.
sections.alternatives:
No alternative action is available, as the City Manager and Council are required by
Ordinance 7,952-N.S. to establish the SSCOC.

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Exhibit A: Ordinance 7,952-N.S, SAFE STREETS Initiative (Adopted December
10, 2024)
Page 4
Page 5 of 20
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ESTABLISHING A MEASURE FF SAFE STREETS CITIZEN OVERSIGHT
COMMITTEE
WHEREAS, Measure FF, also known as the known as the Sustainable and Accountable
Funding for Equitable Street Transformation, Revitalization, Enhancements, and
Essential Traffic Safety (SAFE STREETS) Initiative, was passed by voters on November
5, 2025 and adopted as Ordinance 7,952-N.S. on December 10, 2025, requires the
establishment of the SAFE STREETS Citizen Oversight Committee (SSCOC) in Section
7.11.040; and
WHEREAS, Implementation of SAFE STREETS Initiative will be managed by the
Department of Public Works in alignment with existing City Plans, as detailed in
Ordinance 7,952-N.S.; and
WHEREAS, Requirements for establishing the SSCOC are explicitly laid out in Section
7.11.040 of City Ordinance 7,952-N.S, with a specific membership composition detailed
in Section 7.11.040 (B).
NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED by the City Council of the City of Berkeley that
the SAFE STREETS Citizen Oversight Committee is hereby created.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED, According to Ordinance 7,952-N.S., Section 7.11.040 (B):
1. The SSCOC shall consist of seven members, as follows:
a. Two members of the Transportation and Infrastructure
Commission
b. One member of the Environment and Climate Commission
c. Four members of the public who are Berkeley residents
2. SSCOC members from commissions shall be appointed by their respective bodies,
and members of the public shall be appointed by the City Council as a whole.
Members of the public shall be required to have a background in civil engineering,
transportation engineering, infrastructure program management, or the
implementation of municipal bond or tax measures.
3. The SSCOC annually shall elect one of its members as the chairperson and one
of its members as the vice-chairperson.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the SSCOC is established
